walkability.description: Excellent public transport via Metro and MetroCable.

introText.history: Sustained urban investment turned Medellín into one of Latin America's most celebrated city transformations.
introText.investmentAppeal: Strong rental market with deep expat demand across El Poblado and Laureles.
introText.lifestyle: Coffee culture, year-round outdoor living, and a thriving innovation scene.

neighborhoods.name: El Poblado
neighborhoods.description: Luxury high-rises, international restaurants, and a well-established international community.

neighborhoods.name: Laureles
neighborhoods.description: Tree-lined streets, authentic neighborhood feel, strong long-stay rental demand.

neighborhoods.name: Envigado
neighborhoods.description: Quieter pace, strong community, excellent value for families.

neighborhoods.name: Sabaneta
neighborhoods.description: Traditional pueblo character with metro access and affordable long-stay options.

rentalMarket.shortTermRegulations: Airbnb legal with RNT. 180-day limit proposed.

retireeProfile.languageEase: English common in business and hospitality

introText.history: Colombia's administrative and economic center at 8,660 feet, home to the country's most storied museums, universities, and cultural institutions.
introText.investmentAppeal: Deepest rental market in Colombia — strong executive, student, and family demand across multiple established neighborhoods.
introText.lifestyle: World-class museums, Ciclovía Sundays, and an active restaurant and arts scene.

neighborhoods.name: Chapinero Alto
neighborhoods.description: Cosmopolitan, boutique shopping, and a strong short-term rental market.

neighborhoods.name: Zona G
neighborhoods.description: Gourmet Zone, tree-lined streets, historic luxury residences.

neighborhoods.name: Parque 93
neighborhoods.description: Corporate rentals, manicured parks, and premium mixed-use development.

neighborhoods.name: Cedritos
neighborhoods.description: Newer construction with strong family demand and better value per m².

cities.name: Cartagena

walkability.description: Excellent within the Walled City and Bocagrande.

retireeProfile.languageEase: English common in tourism and hospitality

healthcare.overview: Adequate for routine care with strong private options

introText.history: A UNESCO World Heritage walled city and crown jewel of Colombia's Caribbean coast, with architecture spanning four centuries of history.
introText.investmentAppeal: Tourism-driven rental demand and strong appreciation in the historic center and beachfront corridor.
introText.lifestyle: Caribbean warmth, colorful colonial streets, salsa, and seafood culture.

neighborhoods.name: Centro Histórico
neighborhoods.description: 16th–17th century architecture, vibrant plazas, premium short-term rental potential.

neighborhoods.name: Getsemaní
neighborhoods.description: Street art district with strong appreciation and an evolving arts and café scene.

neighborhoods.name: Bocagrande
neighborhoods.description: High-rise beachfront living with direct Caribbean access and strong vacation rental demand.

neighborhoods.name: Manga
neighborhoods.description: Colorful republican homes in a residential neighborhood with excellent long-stay value.

regulations.foreignOwnership: Historic properties may require ICANH review
regulations.residency: Tourism infrastructure supports multiple visa pathways

first30Days: Catch sunset at Café del Mar

goldenAge.social: Growing European and North American retiree community
